description: Containers without memory limits get OOMKilled or starve neighbors, and without requests get evicted first under node pressure.
body: Failure mode: containers run with no resource requests/limits, so the scheduler cannot reserve capacity, the pod is BestEffort and evicted first under pressure, and a memory leak can consume the node and OOMKill neighbors. Detection signals: OOMKilled status, frequent evictions, node memory pressure, one workload degrading others on the same node. Do not ship production workloads with empty resources. Safer fallback: set requests from observed steady-state usage and memory limits with headroom, choose the right QoS class, and verify no OOMKills under load.
